ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Upma. Nearest parent is a semolina-based cooked porridge. A new concept is recommended.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of upma is 1 bowl (approximately 250g) — a smooth, thick semolina porridge cooked with tempered oil, onion, green chilli, and curry leaves, with a slightly grainy texture from the rava.

nutrient_source.title: Upma — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods, adapted from rava upma preparation data
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN does not have a standalone upma entry. Values are derived from ICMR-NIN semolina (rava) composition data combined with standard upma recipe proportions (rava ~40%, water ~50%, oil ~5%, onion/spices ~5%). Cross-referenced with available recipe-level data from NCC Food and Nutrient Database.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — estimated from semolina (rava) cooked; rava has a moderate GI and the tempering adds fat which slightly lowers GI vs plain boiled semolina.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 500mg per bowl (250g) — moderate; most sodium is from added salt during cooking which can be reduced.
hypertension.guidance: Suitable for hypertensive patients when prepared with reduced salt. Upma is a naturally low-saturated-fat, low-cholesterol dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~66); glycaemic load per full bowl is approximately 39. Semolina has a higher GI than oats but lower than refined white rice.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should limit to 150g and prefer rava upma made with durum wheat semolina over maida-based variants. Adding vegetables (peas, carrot) increases fibre and lowers glycaemic load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Contains mustard seeds and ginger which have anti-inflammatory properties; semolina provides B vitamins including selenium.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis. Not suitable for patients with gluten intolerance (contains wheat semolina). Ghee-based upma provides butyrate benefits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Upma is a popular South Indian breakfast porridge made by dry-roasting semolina (rava/sooji) until fragrant, then adding it to a tempered base of oil, mustard seeds, urad dal, onion, green chilli, ginger, and curry leaves, and cooking with water to a smooth, thick consistency. The texture is fluffy and slightly grainy, with bursts of flavour from the tempering. Common add-ins include peas, cashews, and grated coconut. It is quick to prepare and widely available at South Indian tiffin counters across Singapore. Note: upma contains gluten as it is made from wheat semolina.
common_user_queries: upma vs idli which is better

confidence_reason: No standalone upma entry in ICMR-NIN or HPB Foodo. Values derived from ICMR-NIN rava composition data and standard recipe proportions. Nutrient estimates are consistent with comparable published sources.
notes: Contains wheat gluten (semolina) — not suitable for coeliac or gluten-sensitive patients. Vegan when made with oil; vegetarian when made with ghee (ghee version adds cholesterol ~8mg/100g and dairy allergen). Cholesterol value of 0.0 assumes oil-based preparation.
